  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "staged",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "worn"],
  "percent_complete": 0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Crane hook latch missing or tied back, defeating the safety mechanism designed to prevent load disengagement",
    "Rigging hardware not in compliant working condition prior to lift operations"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the non-compliant hook from service immediately and tag out",
    "Replace with a hook assembly equipped with a properly functioning, self-closing latch that meets ASME B30.10 requirements",
    "Inspect all remaining rigging hardware on site for similar latch defects before any lift begins",
    "Do not commence any hoisting operations until third-party or competent person verification of rigging compliance"
  ],
  "rationale": "A hook with a missing or tied-back latch is a critical rigging defect. The latch is a primary safety device preventing the load from slipping off the hook. Tying it back or operating without it eliminates this safeguard, creating an immediate risk of dropped load, injury, or fatality. ASME B30.10 requires hooks to be equipped with safety latches unless specifically exempted, and OSHA 1926.251 requires all rigging equipment to be inspected and in safe working condition before use. Since work is staged but the rigging configuration is already non-compliant, the condition must be corrected before any lifting activity proceeds.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.10 - Hooks",
    "ASME B30.26 - Rigging Hardware",
    "OSHA 1926.251 - Rigging equipment for material handling"
  ]
}
